display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Is processing
naturally idempotent?
e.g. pure computation

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Can you add an
idempotency key check?

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Does the agent write
to external systems?
e.g. DB, API side-effects

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
At-Least-Once
+ no extra work needed
config: enable.auto.commit=false

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
At-Least-Once
+ idempotency key store
config: enable.auto.commit=false
Redis dedup on task_id

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Exactly-Once Semantics
config: transactional.id
isolation.level=read_committed
~20% throughput cost

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
EOS + Idempotency key
for external write
check-then-act pattern
before each external call
